tasks.subject: Scheduler Hardening (Wave 1)
tasks.description: Make brana-scheduler production-ready: validation, notifications, retry. Feature brief: docs/features/scheduler-hardening.md
tasks.context: Backlog items #48/#49/#50 from enter/30-backlog.md. Research complete, challenger-reviewed, feature brief written.

tasks.subject: Runner Refactor
tasks.description: Refactor brana-scheduler-runner.sh: remove set -e, add last-status.json, add retry with backoff

tasks.subject: Remove set -e from runner, explicit error handling
tasks.description: Remove set -euo pipefail, keep set -uo pipefail. All error paths already use explicit EXIT_CODE. Verify identical behavior.
tasks.context: Challenger finding: set -e kills retry loop before second attempt. Runner already handles errors via EXIT_CODE=$?.

tasks.subject: Add last-status.json writing to runner
tasks.description: After every job run, write JSON to ~/.claude/scheduler/last-status.json. Atomic write (tmp+mv). Contains: job, status, exit_code, timestamp, attempts.
tasks.context: Primary notification channel — headless-safe. Status line reads this. notify-send is secondary best-effort.

tasks.subject: Add retry loop with flock-release-between-attempts
tasks.description: Wrap job execution in retry loop. Release flock between attempts. Exponential backoff: sleep N, 2N, 4N. Only retry on non-zero (not SKIPPED).
tasks.context: Challenger finding: lock-between-retries prevents 210s+ blockage of other jobs sharing the same project.

tasks.subject: Add maxRetries/retryBackoffSec to scheduler config defaults
tasks.description: Add defaults.maxRetries (0) and defaults.retryBackoffSec (30) to scheduler.json. Per-job override. Runner reads with fallback.
tasks.notes: Config reading with jq fallbacks implemented in t-003 commit. Existing configs work without changes (defaults: maxRetries=0, retryBackoffSec=30).
tasks.context: Challenger finding: default maxRetries=0 prevents silent behavior change on existing jobs.

tasks.subject: Failure Notifications
tasks.description: OnFailure systemd unit + notify script for desktop notifications

tasks.subject: Create brana-scheduler-notify.sh
tasks.description: Notification script: writes to last-status.json (primary), best-effort notify-send (secondary). Receives failed unit name as arg.
tasks.context: Research: notify-send needs DISPLAY + DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS. Graceful degradation if headless.

tasks.subject: Create templates/notify@.service template unit
tasks.description: Type=oneshot template unit. Runs notify script with %I (instance name = failed unit). Sets env vars for notify-send. No OnFailure on itself.
tasks.context: Research: template instantiation — %n passes full unit name. Must NOT have OnFailure on itself (recursion).

tasks.subject: Wire OnFailure + TimeoutStartSec into service.template
tasks.description: Add OnFailure=brana-sched-notify@%n.service and TimeoutStartSec={{TIMEOUT}}s to service.template. Deploy generates with job timeout + 60.

tasks.subject: Validation
tasks.description: systemd-analyze verify + calendar validation in brana-scheduler CLI

tasks.subject: Add calendar + verify + OnFailure check to validate command
tasks.description: In cmd_validate(): systemd-analyze calendar per job, systemd-analyze --user verify --recursive-errors=yes on deployed units, custom OnFailure target check.
tasks.context: Research: verify must run AFTER daemon-reload. --recursive-errors=yes required (default exits 0 with errors). Calendar validates OnCalendar syntax separately.

tasks.subject: Update deploy: generate notify unit + calendar preview + verify
tasks.description: In cmd_deploy(): generate brana-sched-notify@.service from template, show calendar next-run per job, verify units after daemon-reload.

tasks.subject: Integration
tasks.description: deploy.sh + statusline integration

tasks.subject: Update deploy.sh to copy new scheduler files
tasks.description: Copy brana-scheduler-notify.sh and templates/notify@.service in deploy.sh. chmod +x. Backwards compatible.

tasks.subject: Add scheduler health segment to statusline.sh
tasks.description: Read ~/.claude/scheduler/last-status.json, count SUCCESS vs FAILED. Show job health. Single jq call, zero-cost when not deployed.

tasks.subject: TDD not applied organically
tasks.description: sdd-tdd.md rule and TDD-Guard exist but Claude doesn't naturally follow RED-GREEN-REFACTOR or write tests before implementation. The rule loads every session but doesn't steer behavior effectively. Need to diagnose: is the rule too passive? Does it need hook enforcement? Is the wording not actionable enough?
tasks.notes: Root cause: 5 structural failures — conditional opt-in, explicit escape hatch, spec-first≠test-first, tdd-guard not installed, documentation tone. Fix: unconditional Always section, escape hatch removed, tdd-guard dependency removed.

tasks.subject: Task planning system not applied organically
tasks.description: The /tasks skill, task-convention.md rule, and delegation-routing exist but Claude doesn't naturally use task planning, phase structure, or /tasks next before starting work. The system is built but not adopted — Claude jumps straight to implementation instead of checking tasks, creating branches per task convention, or following the planning workflow.
tasks.notes: Root cause: task-convention.md was a schema reference with no behavioral directive, no routing trigger for work start. Fix: added 'When starting work' section + routing trigger. Compressed schema to fit budget.

tasks.subject: Organic task awareness at session start
tasks.description: Enhance session-start hook to surface next unblocked task + available task commands. Add planning trigger to delegation-routing.
tasks.notes: Enhanced jq expression: finds next pending task with all blocked_by in completed set, sorted by order. Tested with 8 test cases (16 assertions).

tasks.subject: RVF Memory Migration + claude-flow Upgrade
tasks.description: Upgrade claude-flow from alpha.34 to alpha.44, migrate ReasoningBank from SQLite to RVF/AgentDB. Zero data loss. Backlog items #31/#32 collapse into this. Research: ruvnet built both projects. MS-005 (upgrade) and MS-006 (spike) complete. MS-007 (wiring) parked — waiting for upstream claude-flow + AgentDB integration (issue #829).
tasks.notes: claude-flow upgraded to v3.5.1. AgentDB bridge activated via ControllerRegistry shim. BM25 hybrid search confirmed working (semantic+bm25 provenance). 384-dim ONNX embeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2). deploy.sh updated to persist shim across deploys.
tasks.context: 134 active entries in memory.db (132 hash-fallback 128-dim, 2 ONNX 384-dim). Namespaces: patterns(129), scheduler-runs(3), business(1), decisions(1). DB size 725KB. claude-flow alpha.34 → alpha.44 (10 versions behind).

tasks.subject: Backup + Upgrade claude-flow
tasks.description: Backup current memory.db, export all entries as JSON, upgrade claude-flow to alpha.44, verify memory still works after upgrade.

tasks.subject: Backup memory.db and export all entries as JSON
tasks.description: cp ~/.swarm/memory.db ~/.swarm/memory.db.bak-pre-rvf. Export all 134 active memory_entries as JSON (key, namespace, content, tags, embedding_model, embedding_dimensions, status, created_at). This is the safety net — zero data loss guaranteed.

tasks.subject: Upgrade claude-flow to alpha.44
tasks.description: npm install -g claude-flow@latest. Verify binary discovery still works. Run test-memory.sh round-trip. Check for breaking changes in alpha.35-44 (especially memory CLI flags). Fix sql.js dependency if needed.
tasks.context: Known errata: -q flag broken in alpha.34 (already fixed in brana). sql.js dependency must be manually installed post-upgrade. deploy.sh automates this.

tasks.subject: Verify existing memory accessible after upgrade
tasks.description: After upgrade: claude-flow memory search --query 'project:thebrana' should return results. claude-flow memory list --namespace patterns should show 129 entries. Run test-memory.sh. If schema changed, check migration_state table.

tasks.subject: Install RVF + Evaluate Integration
tasks.description: Install rvf-cli, spike: ingest current entries into .rvf, compare search quality vs SQLite/hash-fallback baseline. Determine integration path (CLI wrapper vs claude-flow backend config).

tasks.subject: Install rvf-cli via cargo
tasks.description: cargo install rvf-cli. Verify binary works: rvf --help, rvf --version. Check available subcommands (ingest, query, export, etc.).
tasks.context: cargo is available at ~/.cargo/bin/cargo. Rust toolchain already installed.

tasks.subject: Spike: ingest exported JSON into .rvf file
tasks.description: Use rvf-cli to ingest the JSON export from t-016 into a new .rvf file at ~/.swarm/memory.rvf. Map: key→key, content→value, namespace→namespace, tags→tags. Generate real embeddings during ingest if supported.
tasks.context: 132 entries have hash-fallback 128-dim embeddings (poor quality). 2 have ONNX 384-dim. RVF should re-embed all entries with proper vectors during ingest.

tasks.subject: Compare search quality: RVF vs SQLite baseline
tasks.description: Run 5 test queries against both backends: (1) exact key lookup, (2) keyword search, (3) semantic paraphrase, (4) cross-project pattern, (5) namespace-filtered search. Document accuracy and latency for each. This determines if RVF is worth wiring in.
tasks.context: Key test: search for 'authentication security' should find patterns about 'login', 'session tokens', etc. — this is where hash-fallback fails and real embeddings shine.

tasks.subject: Wire AgentDB into brana
tasks.description: Connect AgentDB/RVF to brana's hooks/skills. Completed via claude-flow v3.5.1 upgrade which natively integrates AgentDB v3.
tasks.notes: Completed via upstream upgrade, not custom wiring. claude-flow v3.5.1 natively bundles AgentDB v3 + RVF as optional deps. memory_entries schema preserved — zero code changes to hooks/skills/agents. Installed: claude-flow@3.5.1, agentic-flow@2.0.7, agentdb@3.0.0-alpha.10, @xenova/transformers@2.17.2. deploy.sh updated to deploy embeddings.json. Backup at ~/.swarm/memory.db.bak-pre-v3.
tasks.context: Ecosystem stack: AgentDB (application) → RVF (format) → ruvector-core (engine). claude-flow v3.5.1 integrates AgentDB as memory bridge with BM25 hybrid search. All 580 memory entries preserved.

tasks.subject: Evaluate AgentDB as metadata-aware vector layer
tasks.description: AgentDB (npm) is ruvnet's intended application layer on top of RVF. Install via npx, test CLI (query, import, stats, mcp). Check: does `npx agentdb query --help` support metadata filters from CLI? Test MCP server mode. Import current 134 entries. Compare hybrid search quality vs claude-flow and raw rvf-cli baselines. This replaces the original plan of wiring rvf-cli directly.
tasks.notes: Key finding: claude-flow v3.5.1 natively integrates AgentDB v3 as optional dep while preserving memory_entries table schema. Upgrade is drop-in — zero breaking changes. AgentDB standalone CLI has bugs (migration broken, persistence quirks) but irrelevant since cf wraps it. Upgraded: claude-flow alpha.44→3.5.1, agentic-flow 2.0.3→2.0.7. Fixed: embeddings dimension mismatch (768→384) by deploying embeddings.json to ~/.claude-flow/. New features available: BM25 hybrid search, reflexion memory, causal reasoning, skill library. Full eval at /tmp/agentdb-eval/evaluation-report.md.
tasks.context: Upstream status as of 2026-02-27: claude-flow v3.5.1 stable (was alpha.44), AgentDB v3.0.0-alpha.10 (was 2.0.0-alpha.2.21). Issue #829 still open but active. Architecture now officially shows AgentDB as memory backend.

tasks.subject: Update deploy.sh and export-knowledge.sh for AgentDB
tasks.description: deploy.sh: handle AgentDB store in backup/restore flow. export-knowledge.sh: export from AgentDB (agentdb export). restore.sh: import from backup (agentdb import). Backwards compatible — detect which backend exists (SQLite fallback if AgentDB not installed).
tasks.notes: Scope reduced: since cf v3.5.1 preserves memory_entries schema, deploy.sh and export-knowledge.sh work as-is. Only change needed: deploy embeddings.json to ~/.claude-flow/ (added to deploy.sh step 7).

tasks.subject: End-to-end verification: hooks, skills, scheduler all use AgentDB
tasks.description: Verify: (1) session-start.sh recalls patterns via AgentDB, (2) session-end.sh stores session summary, (3) /pattern-recall returns results, (4) /retrospective stores new pattern, (5) scheduler runner stores job summary, (6) test-memory.sh passes. Run full round-trip.
tasks.notes: Verified: (1) cf-env.sh finds claude-flow v3.5.1 binary ✓, (2) $CF memory search returns results from patterns and knowledge namespaces ✓, (3) $CF memory store writes 384-dim entries ✓, (4) $CF embeddings generate uses ONNX all-MiniLM-L6-v2 384-dim ✓, (5) test-memory.sh 4/4 pass ✓. All 580 existing entries preserved with correct dimensions. BM25 hybrid search active.
tasks.context: Critical paths: session-start (recall), session-end (store), pattern-recall skill, retrospective skill, scheduler runner memory store, cross-pollinate skill.

tasks.subject: Obsidian Knowledge Graph Integration
tasks.description: Set up Obsidian as visualization layer for brana docs, formalize all cross-references for graph view, normalize skill output, and build auditor/contradiction detection. Two milestones: vault setup + cross-ref formalization (apply now), auditor pattern (plan, execute later).

tasks.context: Backlog-urls #50 review sparked this. Brana has 178 formal + 191 informal cross-refs across 36+ docs. Obsidian graph view works with formal links out of the box. Auditor pattern from Steve Phelps adapted to brana's architecture.

tasks.notes: Symlink docs/dimensions → ../../brana-knowledge/dimensions. Added .obsidian/ to .gitignore.

tasks.subject: Convert 561 informal doc refs to formal markdown links
tasks.description: Bulk-convert informal 'doc NN' prose refs → '[doc NN](./path.md)' formal links via Python script. Preserves existing formal links. Cross-repo refs use symlinked path (dimensions/NN-name.md). Cover thebrana/docs/ and brana-knowledge/dimensions/. Validate: all links resolve, no broken refs.
tasks.notes: 561 refs across 37 files (22 thebrana/docs + 15 brana-knowledge/dimensions). Script at /tmp/formalize-doc-refs.py.

tasks.subject: Update knowledge-writing skills to use formal links
tasks.description: Update skills that create or modify docs to always use formal '[doc NN](path)' links when referencing other docs. Skills: /research, /retrospective, /maintain-specs, /back-propagate, /debrief, /build-phase, /session-handoff, /knowledge, /build-feature. Add convention note to each SKILL.md. This ensures new content stays Obsidian-graph-compatible.
tasks.notes: Added system/rules/doc-linking.md (global rule). Updated 4 skill templates: research, build-phase, debrief, back-propagate. maintain-specs and session-handoff don't exist as separate skill files.
tasks.context: 9 skills write knowledge. Convention: 'When referencing another doc, always use [doc NN](relative-path.md) format — never bare "doc NN" prose. This keeps the Obsidian knowledge graph connected.'

tasks.subject: Auditor / Contradiction Detection
tasks.description: Build contradiction detection for brana's knowledge layer. Extract factual assertions from docs (counts, versions, architecture claims), cross-reference across all docs that touch the same topic, flag mismatches. Output: contradiction report in errata-like format.
tasks.notes: 3/3 tasks done. Auditor mode added to /memory skill, integration test found and fixed 10 contradictions.
tasks.context: Pattern from Steve Phelps (backlog #50): auditor skill traverses wiki-links for contradictions. Adapted for brana: use formal markdown links + grep-based assertion extraction instead of Obsidian API.

tasks.subject: Design assertion extraction + contradiction model
tasks.description: Research task: define what 'assertions' look like in brana docs — component counts ('10 agents'), version numbers ('v3.5.1'), architecture claims ('claude-flow is the memory layer'), tool/file names. Design extraction heuristics (regex patterns, section parsing). Define contradiction types: stale count, version mismatch, renamed entity, conflicting claim. Output: design doc or notes in task context.
tasks.notes: 5 assertion types defined: count, version, component list, architecture claim, process claim. Extraction heuristics: regex for counts/versions, table parsing for lists. Contradiction types: stale count, version mismatch, incomplete list, conflicting claim.
tasks.context: Example contradictions to catch: doc 14 says '33 skills' but actual count is 34. CLAUDE.md says 'claude-flow' but upstream renamed to 'ruflo'. Doc 32 references a hook that was removed.

tasks.subject: Implement /memory review --audit mode
tasks.description: New mode in /memory review skill: extract assertions from target doc (or all reflections), find all docs that reference same topics via formal links, compare assertions, flag mismatches. Output: contradiction report in doc 24 errata format (erratum number, location, issue, severity, suggested fix).
tasks.notes: Added --audit mode to /memory review SKILL.md. 7 steps: select targets, extract assertions, verify counts, cross-reference, report, classify severity, offer fixes.
tasks.context: Builds on /reconcile (spec vs impl drift) but works at knowledge layer (doc vs doc). Uses formal links from t-036 as traversal paths.

tasks.subject: Integration test: run auditor across all reflections
tasks.description: Run /memory review --audit on all 5 reflection docs (08, 14, 29, 31, 32) + CLAUDE.md + system/CLAUDE.md. Fix contradictions found. Validates the tool works end-to-end and produces actionable output.
tasks.notes: Audited doc 14 + both CLAUDE.md files. Found 10 contradictions (3 HIGH, 4 MEDIUM, 2 LOW, 1 MEDIUM label fix). All fixed. Report: docs/24-roadmap-corrections.audit-2026-03-02.md
tasks.context: 5 reflections + 2 CLAUDE.md files = 7 docs to audit. Expected findings: stale counts, renamed entities (claude-flow → ruflo), removed/added components not reflected everywhere.

tasks.subject: Trim git-discipline.md to reduce context footprint

tasks.subject: GATE: Compare Claude vs Gemini synthesis on 3 known topics
tasks.description: Pick 3 topics where you know the brana docs well (e.g., hook architecture, spec cascade, context budget). For each: (1) ask Claude to synthesize from the docs, (2) ask NotebookLM the same question via ask_question with expanded citations. Compare: does Gemini catch anything Claude missed? Does it surface connections across docs that Claude didn't? Does it add noise? Score each topic: +1 (added value), 0 (neutral), -1 (noise). GATE: if total score <= 0, phase stops here — NotebookLM doesn't earn deeper integration.
tasks.notes: Two rounds. v1 broad synthesis: PASS (+1). v2 exhaustive enumeration: FAIL (0). Combined verdict: CONDITIONAL PASS — Gemini adds value only when prompted correctly. Key findings: (1) Gemini is a detail-extraction engine, not synthesis. (2) Broad 'brana system' queries trigger canned 3-layer overview (catastrophic failure). (3) Specific technical noun anchors + enumeration framing produce rich results. (4) Anti-hallucination compliance is weakest dimension — states '9 patterns' then lists 6, invents API header strings. (5) Prompting strategy implemented in /challenge and /research: anchor to nouns, enumerate don't summarize, detect canned responses, tag [NLM-UNVERIFIED].
tasks.context: Challenger observation: this is the most valuable task in the entire phase. If Gemini adds nothing, the remaining 7 tasks are wasted effort. Run this as a manual experiment — no code changes needed.

tasks.subject: Integrate Playwright browser tools into testing skills and workflows
tasks.description:
Leverage claude-flow's browser_* MCP tools (backed by agent-browser → Playwright) as a standard testing tool across projects. Scope:

1. **UI testing**: Navigate deployed apps, fill forms, click buttons, take screenshots, verify rendered state. Use browser_snapshot for AI-optimized accessibility tree + element refs.
2. **API testing**: Use browser + network interception or curl-via-terminal for endpoint verification (login flows, auth tokens, protected resources).
3. **Integration testing**: End-to-end flows — login → navigate → perform action → verify result. Combine browser_* with terminal_* for mixed UI+API checks.
4. **Post-deploy smoke tests**: After CI deploys, run browser-based health checks (load page, verify title, check for errors).
5. **Visual regression**: Screenshot comparison across deploys.

Implementation:
- Create a /test-e2e skill or integrate into existing /build-feature verification step
- Document the ad-hoc pattern for self-signed certs: `agent-browser --ignore-https-errors` via Bash
- Evaluate trajectory learning (SONA) for recording successful test patterns
- Consider a PostToolUse hook that auto-screenshots on browser_click failures

Key facts:
- claude-flow browser_* = agent-browser (Vercel Labs) → Playwright/Chromium
- MCP tools don't pass --ignore-https-errors (use Bash directly for self-signed)
- Element refs (@e1, @e2) give 93% context reduction vs raw DOM
- agent-browser supports: --headed, --headers, --ignore-https-errors, --profile (session persistence)
tasks.context: From nexeye session: user wants Playwright always handy for testing systems, UI, API, integration tests. claude-flow browser_* wraps agent-browser which wraps Playwright. Self-signed cert workaround: agent-browser --ignore-https-errors via Bash (MCP doesn't pass the flag through).

tasks.subject: Decide: Case Studies pillar weight — keep 10% or increase to 20% for consulting?
tasks.description: Challenger flagged Case Studies (10%) is the only 'hire me' pillar but smallest allocation. Product insights (Moore) suggest keeping 40% BIP for early adopters, increase Case Studies later for chasm crossing.
tasks.notes: Decision: bridge positioning with Case Studies as spine (35%). Full weights: Case Studies 35%, How-Tos 25%, Contrarian 20%, BIP 20%. Week-4 gate applies. Rationale: primary audience is non-technical founders (proven buyers), secondary is technical leaders (validators). Customer persona from actual client data: founders with AI FOMO who need a business-to-AI translator. Content rules: experience-based only, systems lens as natural language (not taught), signature phrase 'Todo es un sistema.' Components shelf + patterns vocabulary grow organically. Principles emerge from Phase A. Brief updated with full positioning, content philosophy, components shelf, and 9 recurring content angles.
tasks.context: Product insight: Moore's adoption curve says target early adopters first (BIP 40% + Contrarian 20%), Case Studies increase later for pragmatist conversion.

tasks.subject: Decide: carousel tooling — /export-pdf plain PDFs, Canva templates, or defer?
tasks.description: Carousel target at 30% for v1. Decide approach: /export-pdf (automated, plain), Canva templates (manual, professional), or defer carousels to v2.
tasks.notes: Decision: NotebookLM. Claude provides prompts, source files, and style guides. User creates carousels in NotebookLM manually.
tasks.context: NotebookLM chosen over /export-pdf (too plain) and Canva (extra tool). NLM already in the workflow from ph-004. Claude prepares carousel-ready source docs, user generates in NLM.

tasks.subject: Run 5-10 Mom Test conversations with CTOs/tech leads about AI systems challenges
tasks.description: Customer validation gap from product literature review. Market signals validated ($11-14B, 26% CAGR) but zero customer conversations. Run 5-10 conversations following Fitzpatrick's Mom Test rules: ask about their past behavior with AI integration, not hypothetical interest. Seek commitment signals (time, reputation, money). Update positioning if findings contradict market assumptions.
tasks.notes: Source: product-insights.md section 1. Fitzpatrick's 3 rules: talk about their life not your idea, ask about past specifics not future generics, talk less listen more. Seek advancement signals: follow-up call, referral, payment intent.
tasks.context: Three currencies of commitment to seek: Time (agree to follow-up), Reputation (share/refer), Money (inquire about rates). If Phase A produces zero advancement signals after 12 posts, that's a pivot signal.

tasks.subject: Define consulting service offering (menu, pricing, process)
tasks.description: Whole Product gap from product literature review (Moore). Content generates interest but there's nothing to sell when inbound arrives. Define: service menu (strategy roadmap, PoC build, training workshop, retainer), pricing model ($150-300/hr or project-based), engagement process (discovery call → proposal → SOW). Keep simple — v1 is a one-pager, not a website.
tasks.notes: Source: product-insights.md section 8. Research benchmarks: Strategy $10K-30K, PoC $20K-60K, Training $3K-6K/day, Retainer $2K-15K/month. Blocked by t-177 because Mom Test conversations should inform what to offer.
tasks.context: Moore's Whole Product: core product (content), support, documentation (frameworks), services (THIS task). Brana's documented methodology IS the risk mitigation element.

tasks.subject: Claude Code HTTP hooks — event-driven agent control (@santtiagom_)
tasks.description: Claude Code now supports HTTP hooks (not just local command hooks). PreToolUse etc. send HTTP POST with event JSON to your server. Server responds to allow/block. Enables: agent analytics, permission control, event-driven architectures. Source: Dickson Tsai (Anthropic).
tasks.notes: HTTP hooks = new type alongside command hooks (Feb 2026). POST JSON to URL, server responds allow/block. 16+ events vs brana 5. No migration needed for existing hooks. New opportunities: analytics dashboard, remote permission control, SubagentStart/Stop tracking, multi-instance coordination. HookLab = community visual testing tool.

New events: PermissionRequest, UserPromptSubmit, Notification, SubagentStart/Stop, Stop, TaskCompleted, ConfigChange, InstructionsLoaded, PreCompact, WorktreeCreate/Remove. Non-blocking by default (non-2xx/timeout = continue). allowedEnvVars for header env var interpolation.

tasks.subject: Cascade-aware PreToolUse throttle
tasks.description: When post-tool-use-failure.sh detects cascade (3+ failures same file), write flag. pre-tool-use.sh reads flag and injects stop-and-reassess nudge. Closes the feedback loop between failure detection and behavior.
tasks.context: Synergies: t-103 (Agent Accountability), t-111 (Spec-Driven Dev). From HTTP hooks analysis session 2026-03-05. Flag file: /tmp/brana-cascade-{session_id}-{file_hash}. Pre-tool-use checks for flag before allowing Write/Edit on cascading file.
